config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama karena tanpa perlindungan terhadap individu, kepentingan masyarakat mudah berubah menjadi alasan untuk menekan orang yang lemah atau minoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai lewat aturan yang adil, bukan dengan mengorbankan hak dasar seseorang. Dalam praktiknya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu-individu yang haknya dihormati.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual’s rights. From an American perspective, that idea makes me nervous because history shows how easily “the common good” can be used to justify trampling minorities, dissidents, or unpopular people. Society absolutely needs rules and responsibilities, but those should usually be limited by basic rights like free speech, due process, and bodily autonomy. In my view, a healthy society is one that protects individuals first and asks people to sacrifice only when there is a clear, narrowly defined public necessity.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman saya melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal yang benar justru masyarakat kuat kalau hak dasar orang dijaga dulu. Tentu ada keadaan darurat atau kebutuhan publik tertentu, tetapi itu harus sangat terbatas, transparan, dan tidak boleh jadi alasan untuk menginjak minoritas atau orang yang tidak berdaya. Jadi, prioritasnya menurut saya bukan masyarakat di atas individu, melainkan keseimbangan yang menempatkan hak individu sebagai pagar utama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an American point of view, that phrase sounds too open-ended and too easy to abuse, because governments and majorities often claim "society" while really protecting power, convenience, or majority preferences. I am fine with limited exceptions for real emergencies, public safety, or preventing direct harm, but those exceptions need strict limits and cannot become a blank check to override basic rights. In my view, a free society is strongest when it treats individual rights as the default and justifies any restriction with clear, narrow necessity.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus mengalahkan hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Di Indonesia, saya justru sering melihat istilah “demi kepentingan bersama” dipakai terlalu longgar, sehingga yang terjadi bukan perlindungan publik, melainkan tekanan pada orang kecil, minoritas, atau yang tidak punya suara. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang jelas; kalau tidak, masyarakat mudah berubah jadi alat pembenaran bagi kekuasaan atau mayoritas. Jadi menurut saya, yang benar bukan mendahulukan masyarakat di atas individu, melainkan menjaga hak individu sebagai batas utama sambil tetap mengatur kepentingan umum secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. From an American perspective, that kind of statement is too broad and too easy for majorities or governments to abuse, because "society" often ends up meaning whoever already has power. I am fine with limited exceptions for real emergencies or direct harm, but those exceptions need strict limits and cannot become a blanket excuse to override basic rights. In my view, a free society is healthiest when individual rights are the default and any restriction has to be clearly justified, narrow, and temporary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
